denial_pattern_sources: ignored_physician_letter: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey
unreasonable_documentation_deadline: DOL ERISA Claims Procedure Regulations 29 CFR § 2560.503-1(g); AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Survey
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 10/16/2026 for Psychiatric partial hospitalization related to Panic disorder with agoraphobia (F40.01). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The duration of Psychiatric partial hospitalization exceeds plan limits without new clinical review. Continuation requires updated documentation not received.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with duration excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 67-year-old member has Panic disorder with agoraphobia (F40.01). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Psychiatric partial hospitalization recommended as the next appropriate step. Multiple ER visits in 2025; lorazepam taper completed; SSRIs partially effective only. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that duration excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
